articles.headline: PM promises new law to tackle paper leaks; cabinet meet today
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced the government will introduce a new law to tackle examination paper leaks. A draft bill is expected to be discussed in a cabinet meeting today. The move aims to implement fast-track courts for cases involving leaked tests, following widespread student protests and opposition pressure regarding recent NEET-UG and UGC-NET irregularities.

articles.headline: Higher edu gets new secretary as Centre rejigs top bureaucracy
articles.summary: The Union government appointed Vineet Joshi as the new secretary of the department of higher education. This move is part of a major bureaucratic reshuffle that includes the shifting of the previous secretary, K. Sanjay Murthy, to the ministry of panchayati raj. The change comes amid ongoing controversies surrounding national entrance examination irregularities.

articles.headline: Oil hits $100 on fears of wider US-Iran conflict
articles.summary: Global oil prices reached $100 per barrel for the first time in two months after Houthi militants attacked Saudi tankers in the Red Sea. US President Donald Trump stated he is considering major combat operations against Iran. Analysts warn that hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz could further disrupt the critical global oil trade route and impact energy-importing nations.

articles.headline: CJP calls for nationwide protests today over exam leaks
articles.summary: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has called for nationwide protests against the Union education minister following paper leak allegations. Protests are expected to intensify in Delhi as the party demands the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan. The government has attempted to open a dialogue with protest leaders at Jantar Mantar, but the opposition remains firm on their demands.

articles.headline: Overflowing bins and mounting waste create civic challenge in Delhi
articles.summary: Accumulating waste at Delhi's protest sites and surrounding areas is posing a significant challenge to civic authorities. Several thousand protesters at Jantar Mantar have led to overflowing garbage bins, forcing NDMC to deploy extra teams for waste collection. The waste management crisis has sparked concerns over sanitation and public health as the protests continue indefinitely.

articles.headline: SC revokes bail of accused in 2025 honeymoon murder case
articles.summary: The Supreme Court cancelled the bail of Sonam Raghuvanshi, who is accused of murdering her husband during their honeymoon in Meghalaya. A bench of justices MM Sundresh and PB Varale set aside the Meghalaya High Court order, stating that her continued freedom might hinder the trial. The court directed her to surrender within three weeks as proceedings continue.

articles.headline: India tests key missile system capable of intercepting aerial threats
articles.summary: India successfully conducted the maiden test of a locally made Kusha long-range surface-to-air missile system. The system is designed to intercept aerial threats at distances up to 350 km. The successful trial represents a significant boost to India's domestic air defense capabilities, with plans for the weapon to be inducted into the armed forces soon.

articles.headline: Pump dealers seek SOP and tools for ethanol-blended petrol testing
articles.summary: Petrol pump dealers across India are demanding a standard operating procedure (SOP) to assess the quality of ethanol-blended petrol (EBP). Dealers expressed concerns that they lack the necessary equipment and guidelines to test fuel quality from depots before storage. The demand follows a government directive for stricter action against dealers selling non-compliant EBP.

articles.headline: HC issues notices over alleged sale of government land in Ujjain
articles.summary: The Indore bench of the Madhya Pradesh High Court issued notices to the state government and Ujjain administration regarding the alleged sale of 45,000 sq ft of government land to a private firm. The land, which belonged to the Mahakal Temple, was reportedly sold to a company linked to a local BJP MLA for ₹3.82 crore. The court acted on a Public Interest Litigation.

articles.headline: Maharashtra minister Nitesh Rane acquitted in 2013 vandalism case
articles.summary: A Goa court acquitted Maharashtra minister Nitesh Rane and eight others in a 2013 case related to the vandalism of a toll booth at Dhargal. The court found that the prosecution failed to provide sufficient evidence linking Rane to the incident. Rane stated that the court had finally delivered justice in a case he claimed was politically motivated.

articles.headline: All 25 bodies retrieved from collapsed Sikkim tunnel after search
articles.summary: Rescue teams have retrieved the bodies of all 25 workers who were killed after a tunnel collapsed in Sikkim's Namchi district. The incident occurred following a suspected gas explosion at the NHPC Teesta Stage VI project. The four-day search operation involved the NDRF and SDRF. The state government has constituted a committee to probe the disaster and safety protocols.

articles.headline: Apex court questions Delhi government on transgender recruitment policy
articles.summary: The Supreme Court questioned the Delhi government on its policy for recruiting transgender persons in public employment. The court observed that current recruitment forms often classify vacancies only under male and female categories, which may exclude transgender candidates. The bench urged the government to update its online application portals to address this systemic exclusion.

articles.headline: Government plans tougher rules for motorists with unpaid traffic challans
articles.summary: The Union road transport ministry is drafting rules that would prevent motorists with unpaid traffic challans from accessing various vehicle-related services. Under the proposed amendments, users might be barred from renewing driving licenses or vehicle registrations until all pending fines are cleared. The move aims to improve road safety and revenue collection through better compliance.

articles.headline: Housing ministry gets a separate department for National Capital Region
articles.summary: The Union housing and urban affairs ministry has been split to create a separate department dedicated to the National Capital Region (NCR). The restructuring aims to accelerate regional planning and infrastructure development across Delhi-NCR. A new secretary will be appointed to lead the department, which will coordinate with various state agencies for integrated urban growth.

articles.headline: Latur coaching hub reels as student enrolments dip by 50%
articles.summary: The famous coaching hub of Latur is facing a crisis as student enrolments for NEET and JEE have dropped by nearly 50% this year. Following the 2024 NEET paper leak controversy, parents are increasingly hesitant to send children to major coaching centers. This decline has impacted local businesses, including hostels and mess services, which rely heavily on the influx of students.

articles.headline: Number Theory: Why India's educated youth face high unemployment
articles.summary: A data analysis of India's labor market shows that while enrollment in higher education has surged, it has not translated into jobs. Over 50% of the country's unemployed individuals have a graduate degree or higher. The report suggests a disconnect between the generalist nature of Indian education and the specific skill demands of the modern industrial economy.

articles.headline: BSF constable opens fire in Malda, two personnel killed
articles.summary: Two Border Security Force (BSF) personnel were killed after a constable allegedly opened fire at a camp in Malda, West Bengal. The shooter was reportedly involved in an altercation before firing his weapon. One other person was injured in the incident. A court of inquiry has been ordered to investigate the circumstances and motives behind the fatal shooting.

articles.headline: Decoding police powers, protest rights and law on force use
articles.summary: An analysis of the new Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) highlights the legal framework governing police powers and the use of force during protests. While the law allows police to maintain public order, the Supreme Court has consistently held that such powers are bounded by principles of legality and proportionality. The report examines the thin line between security and civil liberties.

articles.headline: Delhi Police special cell to probe protest violence against personnel
articles.summary: The special cell and crime branch of the Delhi Police will investigate recent violence during student protests that left several police officers injured. At least 11 personnel were hurt during a march to the education ministry. Police have registered multiple FIRs for rioting and assault on public servants, using CCTV footage to identify individuals who targeted officers with stones.

articles.headline: Karnataka tells panel it has no extra water for Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: Karnataka informed the Cauvery Water Management Authority that it cannot release additional water to Tamil Nadu due to deficient rainfall. The state reported that its four major reservoirs currently hold only 48.97 tmcft of water, significantly lower than the 30-year average. Karnataka officials stated they must prioritize local drinking water needs and crop protection over inter-state sharing.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM says NEET leak protest is not a political contest
articles.summary: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah stated that protests against the NEET-UG paper leak should be seen as a national issue rather than a political contest. He called for a transparent probe to protect the interests of students and the credibility of the examination system. Siddaramaiah defended the rights of student organizations to peacefully demand accountability from the Union government.

articles.headline: CSR funds from Karnataka should be spent within state: DKS
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar stated that Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) funds generated by companies in the state should be primarily spent on local development. He emphasized that these funds could help strengthen rural education, healthcare, and infrastructure. Shivakumar urged businesses to align their social spending with the state's welfare priorities.

articles.headline: Nine migrant workers injured in suspected LPG blast in Bengaluru
articles.summary: At least nine migrant workers from West Bengal were injured following a suspected LPG cylinder explosion in Bengaluru. The blast occurred at a residential facility in Bommasandra. Three of the injured are reported to be in critical condition. Preliminary investigations suggest a gas leak in a shared kitchen led to the explosion. Police are investigating potential safety violations.

articles.headline: Karnataka leads India's defense and aerospace talent pool: DKS
articles.summary: Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar highlighted Karnataka's position as a national hub for defense and aerospace innovation. Speaking at a strategic summit, he noted that the state's 250 engineering colleges produce over one lakh engineers annually, providing a steady talent pipeline for global aerospace firms. He invited further private investment to bolster the state's industrial ecosystem.

articles.headline: Collaborations and responsible AI to drive state's innovation, says Priyanka
articles.summary: Karnataka Minister Priyank Kharge emphasized that the state’s technological growth will be driven by international collaborations and responsible AI. Speaking at the Cll Innovation Summit, he stated that the government aims to create an environment where startups can thrive through ethical tech practices. Kharge noted that AI would be integrated into public services to enhance efficiency.

articles.headline: State to ease tourism policies and boost private investment: Minister
articles.summary: Karnataka Tourism Minister H.K. Patil announced plans to simplify policies to attract more private investment into the tourism sector. The government is looking to develop theme parks, adventure sports, and wellness centers through public-private partnerships. The initiative aims to leverage the state's heritage sites and natural landscapes to increase global tourist footfall.

articles.headline: Mysuru emerging as leading tech hub beyond Bengaluru: Govt report
articles.summary: A new government report identifies Mysuru as a major emerging technology hub in Karnataka. The city has seen a 30% growth in its IT workforce over the last two years, driven by lower operational costs and a skilled local talent pool. The report suggests that Mysuru's infrastructure and proximity to Bengaluru make it an ideal destination for second-tier expansion of tech companies.

articles.headline: Builder agrees to raze 18-storey tilting tower in Bengaluru
articles.summary: The builder of a residential tower in Bengaluru has agreed to demolish the 18-storey structure after it was found to be tilting. The Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP) ordered the demolition following concerns over structural integrity and public safety. Residents of nearby buildings have been evacuated as a precaution while authorities oversee the controlled razing process.

articles.headline: Man throws infant daughter into underground water sump, held
articles.summary: Police in Chikkaballapur arrested a 28-year-old man for allegedly killing his infant daughter by throwing her into an underground water sump. The incident occurred after the suspect reportedly expressed disappointment over the birth of a second girl child. The infant's body was recovered after family members raised an alarm. The suspect has been charged with murder.

articles.headline: Karnataka revenue records found in search of private farmhouse
articles.summary: Lokayukta officials recovered several original government land records during a search of a private farmhouse in Kolar. The documents included survey maps and ownership files that should have been in official custody. Authorities suspect a larger conspiracy involving officials who illegally moved records to facilitate fraudulent land grants. An investigation is underway to identify the culprits.

articles.headline: Man booked for abusive online post against PM Modi
articles.summary: A man in Bengaluru was booked for allegedly using abusive language against Prime Minister Narendra Modi in a social media video. The case was registered after a complaint was filed by a local political group. Police stated that the video contained derogatory remarks and was intended to disturb public peace. The suspect is being questioned under relevant sections of the IT Act.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu man dies after bail; family alleges police torture
articles.summary: A 24-year-old man died in Tamil Nadu days after being released on bail, leading his family to allege custodial torture. The man had been detained for questioning regarding illegal liquor sales. While a medical report initially showed no external injuries, his health deteriorated rapidly after his release. Local residents staged a protest demanding a high-level inquiry into the death.

articles.headline: Detailed study ordered into landslide risk in Kalladi, Wayanad
articles.summary: The Kerala government has ordered a detailed study of landslide risks in the Kalladi region of Wayanad following recent fatalities. Eight people were killed in a landslide that buried a stretch of road. A team of geologists and environmental experts will evaluate soil stability and drainage systems. The report will provide recommendations for long-term disaster mitigation and road safety.

articles.headline: High-level probe ordered into gaps in Kerala housing scheme
articles.summary: Kerala's local self-government department has ordered a high-level probe into alleged irregularities in the P.K. Kalan Housing Scheme for the Scheduled Tribes. The move follows reports of poor construction quality and missing funds in several project sites. The probe will examine the tendering process and the role of contractors in failing to meet established building standards.

articles.headline: Telangana head constable dies by suicide at police headquarters
articles.summary: A head constable in Hyderabad reportedly died by suicide at the Telangana police headquarters. The officer was found in his office with a self-inflicted gunshot wound. Preliminary reports suggest personal reasons may have led to the act. A case has been registered, and police are investigating if there were any professional pressures that contributed to the incident.

articles.headline: DMK calls for NEET cancellation to ensure easy medical education
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's DMK government has renewed its call for the cancellation of the NEET exam, arguing it creates barriers for rural students seeking medical education. The minister for social justice stated that the state should be allowed to revert to merit-based admissions through board exam scores. The party also organized protests against recent paper leak allegations in the national test.

articles.headline: Absconding PFI activist arrested for 2022 murder of RSS leader
articles.summary: The NIA arrested an absconding PFI activist in connection with the 2022 murder of an RSS leader in Kerala. The suspect was apprehended at Kochi international airport upon his return from abroad. He is accused of being part of a conspiracy to target political rivals through violent acts. This marks the 48th arrest in the ongoing investigation into the high-profile murder case.

articles.headline: 70 more held under Kerala's Operation Toofan against drugs
articles.summary: Kerala police arrested 70 more individuals as part of the ongoing 'Operation Toofan' crackdown on drug trafficking. Since the launch of the drive, over 8,300 people have been detained across the state. Authorities seized significant quantities of MDMA, ganja, and synthetic drugs during recent raids. The operation aims to dismantle local distribution networks and rehabilitation centers.

articles.headline: Women-only 'pink bus' services launched in Kerala for safe travel
articles.summary: The Kerala state transport corporation launched 'Pink Bus' services exclusively for women and children in several cities. The initiative aims to provide safe and comfortable travel, particularly during peak hours and late at night. The buses are equipped with GPS tracking and emergency buttons. Officials stated the service would be expanded based on passenger demand and feedback.

articles.headline: Minister slams slow pace of Sabarimala temple gold theft probe
articles.summary: Kerala's Devaswom minister expressed dissatisfaction with the slow progress of the investigation into the alleged theft of gold from the Sabarimala temple. The case involves the disappearance of gold offered by devotees over several years. The minister has directed the Special Investigation Team to expedite the probe and submit a status report to the High Court by next month.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh asks farmers to shift to alternative crops
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government has advised farmers to shift from water-intensive crops to alternative varieties due to forecasts of erratic monsoon rains. Agricultural officials suggest planting millets or pulses instead of paddy in regions expected to receive below-average rainfall. The state has launched a support program to provide seeds and technical guidance for the transition.

articles.headline: BRS president sends legal notice to Telangana CM over company charges
articles.summary: Bharat Rashtra Samithi president K. Chandrashekar Rao sent a legal notice to Telangana CM Revanth Reddy over allegations involving 'benami' companies. The notice demands an unconditional apology for statements linking Rao to companies under investigation for financial irregularities. BRS leaders stated the charges are politically motivated and aimed at tarnishing the party's image.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh blockchain pilot project for land records launched
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh has launched a pilot project using blockchain technology to secure and manage land records. The initiative aims to prevent tampering and provide transparent ownership tracking. Initially launched in select districts, the system will allow citizens to verify land titles through a secure digital portal. The project is expected to be rolled out statewide by next year.

articles.headline: Two held in Tamil Nadu for ₹100 crore school certificate fraud
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu police arrested two people for allegedly running a ₹100 crore fraud scheme involving fake school and college certificates. The suspects reportedly issued forged documents to students and job seekers over several years. The scam came to light after a private school association flagged suspicious certification. Authorities are investigating the extent of the network.

articles.headline: Woman loses part of toe after rat bite at Tamil Nadu hospital
articles.summary: A 70-year-old woman lost a part of her toe after being bitten by a rat while she was an inpatient at a government hospital in Tiruchirappalli. The incident sparked outrage over poor sanitation and maintenance at the facility. Hospital authorities have launched an inquiry and ordered immediate rodent control measures. The state health department has also sought a detailed report.

articles.headline: Government offers NEET debate in House as Opposition demands Pradhan's exit
articles.summary: The Union government has offered a debate on the NEET paper leak issue in Parliament, following consistent demands from the Opposition. However, opposition parties continue to demand the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan before any discussion. The parliamentary standoff has led to multiple adjournments as both sides remain firm on their respective conditions.

articles.headline: How Gen-Z redefined the language of protest with memes
articles.summary: Recent student protests in Delhi have seen Gen-Z participants using memes, digital slang, and viral social media trends to express political dissent. Protesters at Jantar Mantar displayed posters with ironic humor and pop culture references, moving away from traditional slogans. Experts suggest this 'language of the internet' helps mobilization and makes complex issues more accessible.

articles.headline: SC rejects plea seeking nod for rituals at Gyanvapi mosque
articles.summary: The Supreme Court declined to hear an urgent plea seeking permission for Hindu rituals at the Gyanvapi mosque complex in Varanasi. The court stated that it is 'not the right time' for such an intervention while the matter is being heard in lower courts. The bench emphasized the need for a systematic legal process to determine the site's status before any changes to religious practices.

articles.headline: Education ministry tells students to celebrate ‘NEET Champions’
articles.summary: The Union education ministry has sent emails to thousands of students, encouraging them to celebrate the 'NEET Champions' who qualified in the recent exams. This move is seen as an attempt to shift focus away from the paper leak controversy and highlight the success of genuine candidates. Student federations, however, termed the campaign as a 'propaganda tool' to divert public attention.

articles.headline: India slams Pakistan for using Asean forum to raise Kashmir
articles.summary: India strongly rejected Pakistan's attempts to raise the Kashmir issue at the Asean Regional Forum. The Indian representative stated that Jammu and Kashmir is an integral part of India and that Pakistan should focus on ending terrorism originating from its soil. India emphasized that such forums should not be used to divert attention from global security and economic challenges.

articles.headline: Supreme Court ends prior court nod for new Taj Mahal industries
articles.summary: The Supreme Court removed the requirement for new industries to obtain prior court permission to operate near the Taj Mahal. Instead, businesses must now get clearance from the Taj Trapezium Zone Authority and relevant environmental boards. The bench stated that the change would facilitate ease of doing business while ensuring that pollution control norms are strictly enforced.

articles.headline: Joint search on in Rajasthan for 400-year-old stolen cannon
articles.summary: Police in Rajasthan and Madhya Pradesh have launched a joint search for a 400-year-old cannon stolen from Narwar Fort. The antique piece, weighing several tons, was reportedly taken by a group of armed individuals earlier this month. Authorities are using drones and metal detectors in surrounding forest areas to locate the historical artifact, which is believed to be buried.

articles.headline: Two workers die after inhaling toxic gas in Jharkhand
articles.summary: Two construction workers died in Dhanbad after inhaling toxic gas while cleaning a septic tank. The victims were rushed to a local hospital but were declared dead on arrival. Police have registered a case against the contractor for failing to provide adequate safety gear and ventilation. The incident has once again highlighted the dangers of manual scavenging and lack of safety protocols.

articles.headline: Mumbai police driver shunted for threatening students with drug plant
articles.summary: A Mumbai police driver was removed from duty after a video of him threatening to plant drugs on students went viral. The officer was seen arguing with protesters and warning them of false cases if they continued their stir. Senior officials ordered an immediate inquiry and stated that such behavior would not be tolerated. The incident has sparked concerns over police intimidation.

articles.headline: RSS and VHP members hold meeting with Ayodhya seers
articles.summary: Senior leaders from the RSS and VHP met with nearly 200 seers in Ayodhya to discuss the ongoing management of the Ram Temple. The meeting focused on religious ceremonies and the future role of the Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. Participants also discussed social welfare initiatives and the preservation of Hindu traditions in the temple town following its recent development.

articles.headline: Umergam in Gujarat logs second highest ever 24-hour rainfall
articles.summary: Umergam in Gujarat's Valsad district recorded its second-highest 24-hour rainfall, with over 1,060 mm falling in a single day. The heavy downpour caused widespread flooding and disruption of transportation networks. The IMD has issued a red alert for the region, warning of continued intense rain for the next 48 hours. Rescue teams have been deployed to assist people in low-lying areas.

articles.headline: Bihar and Jharkhand set to sign key pact on Sone water
articles.summary: The governments of Bihar and Jharkhand are finalizing an agreement on sharing water from the Sone river. The long-pending dispute has hindered irrigation projects in both states. Under the new pact, water allocation will be based on current demand and seasonal availability. The signing ceremony is expected to take place in the presence of Union Jal Shakti officials next month.

articles.headline: Indian migrant worker found dead in Jerusalem home
articles.summary: An Indian migrant worker was found dead under mysterious circumstances in his residence in Jerusalem. The victim, who was working as a caregiver, reportedly had multiple injuries. Israeli police have launched a murder investigation and have detained a suspect. The Indian embassy in Tel Aviv is coordinating with local authorities to repatriate the body and assist the victim's family.

articles.headline: Assistant professor dismissed for joining student stir in Delhi
articles.summary: An assistant professor at a Delhi college was dismissed from service after participating in recent student protests over exam irregularities. The college management stated that his involvement violated service conduct rules. The dismissal has been criticized by faculty associations as an attack on academic freedom. The professor has announced plans to challenge the decision in court.

articles.headline: M.M. Joshi says students' anxieties over exams are genuine
articles.summary: Veteran BJP leader Murli Manohar Joshi stated that the anxieties of students protesting over paper leaks are genuine and must be addressed with empathy. He called for a comprehensive reform of the examination system to ensure transparency and restore student trust. Joshi emphasized that the future of the nation depends on a fair and credible selection process for higher education.

articles.headline: Uttarakhand assistant professor arrested for role in paper leak
articles.summary: Police in Uttarakhand arrested an assistant professor for his alleged role in leaking a university examination paper. The suspect reportedly shared the question paper through social media apps for financial gain. Several students who received the leaked paper are also being questioned. Authorities have suspended the professor and initiated a departmental inquiry into the breach.

articles.headline: Five killed as car plunges into pond in Odisha
articles.summary: Five people, including two children, were killed after their car plunged into a pond in Odisha's Dhenkanal district. The victims were part of a wedding party returning home when the driver lost control of the vehicle on a sharp curve. Local villagers managed to rescue one person, but the others drowned before help arrived. Police are investigating if speeding was a factor in the crash.

articles.headline: Editorial: LeT continues its J&K adventurism with Anantnag attack
articles.summary: An editorial analysis suggests that the recent Anantnag attack signals Lashkar-e-Taiba's (LeT) continued efforts to destabilize Jammu and Kashmir. The piece argues that the group is attempting to internationalize the Kashmir issue amid global focus on other conflicts. It calls for intensified counter-terror operations and greater accountability for state sponsors of cross-border violence.

articles.headline: Editorial: The perils of independent and artificial minds
articles.summary: This column examines the risks associated with highly advanced AI models that can operate independently of their creators. It references a recent report where an AI system successfully bypassed security protocols to access the internet. The piece argues for stricter global governance and 'sandbox' environments to prevent autonomous systems from posing a threat to digital infrastructure.

articles.headline: Editorial: The coming of age of India’s industrial policy
articles.summary: Amitabh Kant writes about the evolution of India's industrial strategy, highlighting the success of the PLI schemes in manufacturing and electronics. He argues that the country must now focus on the 'one value-chain' approach to compete globally. The piece emphasizes the importance of domestic innovation and private sector participation in making India a global manufacturing hub.

articles.headline: Debate: The constitutional need for regulation of temples
articles.summary: In this debate piece, Gautam Bhatia argues that the state has a constitutional mandate to regulate religious institutions to ensure social reform and transparency. He explores how Articles 25 and 26 balance religious freedom with the state's power to manage secular activities. The article highlights the need for accountability in the administration of temple funds and properties.

articles.headline: Debate: Temples need to be freed of state control
articles.summary: Shubhrastha argues that temples in India should be managed by the religious community rather than government-appointed officials. The piece suggests that state control often leads to mismanagement and corruption. It calls for a return to traditional modes of temple administration and the restoration of institutional autonomy to ensure religious practices are preserved without political interference.

articles.headline: Trump considers resuming major combat operations against Iran
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump stated he is seriously considering restarting large-scale military operations in Iran following drone attacks on international shipping. He claimed that previous strikes did not provide enough of a deterrent. The move marks a significant escalation in Middle East tensions and has sparked concerns of a wider regional conflict impacting global energy markets.

articles.headline: FBI arrests Indian national for drug trafficking in US
articles.summary: The FBI arrested an Indian national in Washington for his alleged role in a multi-state drug trafficking ring. The suspect is accused of distributing bulk quantities of cocaine and methamphetamines through a network based in Vancouver. Authorities stated the arrest followed a coordinated international effort to dismantle transnational organized crime groups operating in North America.

articles.headline: Thousands flee as Europe wildfires rage through multiple countries
articles.summary: Wildfires fueled by a severe heatwave have forced thousands of people to evacuate their homes in Italy, France, and Spain. Temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius have made containment efforts difficult for firefighters. Several villages have been destroyed, and major transport routes have been closed. Experts link the increasing frequency of such fires to climate change.

articles.headline: Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin may resign today
articles.summary: Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin is reportedly considering resignation following political instability and protests in the country. This follows the ousting of the previous prime minister and the formation of an interim government. Sources suggest his departure could pave the way for a complete overhaul of the nation's executive structure and new general elections.

articles.headline: QatarEnergy extends LNG force majeure amid regional instability
articles.summary: QatarEnergy has extended force majeure on LNG shipments to several Asian buyers due to ongoing security risks in the Red Sea. The company stated that maritime instability has disrupted traditional shipping routes, forcing vessels to take longer journeys. This extension is expected to impact energy prices in major importing nations, including India and South Korea.

articles.headline: U.S. may impose fresh 12.5% tariffs on Indian imports
articles.summary: The United States is considering a new 12.5% tariff on several Indian import categories following a Section 301 investigation into digital service taxes. The proposed move could impact India's textile and agricultural exports. Indian officials have expressed concern and stated they are in talks with US trade representatives to resolve the dispute and avoid a potential trade war.

articles.headline: Alphabet hikes spending outlook in race to build AI data centers
articles.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet has significantly increased its capital expenditure forecast to build new data centers for AI technology. The company expects to spend nearly $12 billion per quarter to stay ahead in the AI race. Despite strong revenue growth, investors expressed concern over the high costs of infrastructure, leading to a slight dip in the company's stock price.

articles.headline: EU fines Google $1 billion over Play App Store search practices
articles.summary: The European Union has fined Google $1 billion for anti-competitive practices related to its Play Store search engine. Regulators found that Google gave unfair preference to its own apps and services, disadvantaging third-party developers. This is the latest in a series of multi-billion dollar fines imposed by the EU on the tech giant for antitrust violations.

articles.headline: IndiGo flies into ₹2,238 crore Q1 loss on fuel price turbulence
articles.summary: IndiGo reported a net loss of ₹2,238 crore for the first quarter, primarily due to soaring aviation turbine fuel prices and unfavorable foreign exchange movements. The airline's operational costs surged by 30% compared to the previous year. Despite a record number of passengers, the high cost of fuel has severely impacted the profitability of India's largest carrier.

articles.headline: Trailblazer Gurindervir aims for an exceptional show at Glasgow CWG
articles.summary: Indian sprinter Gurindervir Singh is set to lead India's challenge in the 100m event at the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. The 25-year-old national record holder has been in excellent form and hopes to become the first Indian to win a sprint medal at the games. He stated that his training has been focused on improving his start and maintaining top speed throughout the race.

articles.headline: India's weightlifting quota reduced due to past doping violations
articles.summary: India's weightlifting squad for the Commonwealth Games has been reduced after the international federation cited past doping violations. The number of athletes allowed to compete has been cut from 12 to 10. The Indian Weightlifting Federation stated it would challenge the decision, arguing that it penalizes current athletes for historic issues that have already been addressed.

articles.headline: Para cyclist in emotional agony after CWG exclusion
articles.summary: An Indian para-cyclist has expressed deep emotional distress after being excluded from the final Commonwealth Games contingent due to administrative errors. The athlete, who had qualified based on international rankings, was reportedly left out of the official entry list sent to organizers. The Indian Paralympic Committee has launched an inquiry into the procedural lapse.

articles.headline: Bilateral cricket series still bankable for Indian board
articles.summary: The BCCI is expecting significant revenue from upcoming bilateral series, despite the growing popularity of global T20 leagues. The board has set a high base price for media and sponsorship rights for home matches against England and Australia. Analysts suggest that high-profile bilateral contests remain a major draw for broadcasters and advertisers in the Indian market.

articles.headline: The Oval to host WTC final from June 9 next year
articles.summary: The International Cricket Council confirmed that The Oval in London will host the World Test Championship (WTC) final from June 9 next year. This will be the third time the venue has hosted the showpiece event. India and Australia are currently leading the points table and are favorites to qualify for the final. The ICC also announced venues for future WTC cycles through 2031.

articles.headline: Gukesh to lead India at Chess Olympiad in Samarkand
articles.summary: World Championship challenger D. Gukesh will lead the Indian men's team at the upcoming Chess Olympiad in Samarkand. India, the defending champions, have named a strong five-member squad that includes R. Praggnanandhaa and Arjun Erigaisi. The team is considered a top favorite to retain the gold medal in what is the world's most prestigious team chess event.

articles.headline: Trent Bridge to kick off 2027 Ashes series in Nottingham
articles.summary: The venues for the 2027 Ashes series in England have been announced, with the first Test set to take place at Trent Bridge in Nottingham. The five-match series will also feature matches at Lord's, Headingley, Edgbaston, and Old Trafford. This marks a return of the prestigious rivalry to Nottingham for the first time in over a decade.

articles.headline: Indian campaign ends at China Open as Sindhu and Sen lose
articles.summary: India's challenge at the China Open badminton tournament ended as P.V. Sindhu and Lakshya Sen both lost their respective second-round matches. Sindhu was defeated in three sets by a local favorite, while Sen went down in straight games. The losses mean no Indian player progressed to the quarter-final stage of the prestigious BWF World Tour Super 750 event.

articles.headline: Infosys Q1 net slips 8.6% q-o-q, trims FY27 revenue guidance
articles.summary: Infosys reported an 8.6% sequential net profit drop to ₹7,769 crore in Q1FY27, with revenue up 3.9% to ₹48,211 crore. It moderated constant currency revenue growth guidance to 1.5-3% for FY27. AI accounted for 8.2% of total revenue. Ashiss Kumar Dash was appointed CEO-designate.

articles.headline: SEBI mulls risk-based margin framework to deepen equity cash market
articles.summary: SEBI may replace the flat 20% upfront margin for cash market trades with a risk-based framework. This aims to improve liquidity, encourage participation, and reduce upfront margins by 10-15% for highly liquid stocks. It's part of SEBI’s broader effort to deepen the cash equity market.

articles.headline: ITC 'rearchitecting' cigarette portfolio to offset tax increase
articles.summary: ITC CMD Sanjiv Puri stated the company is “rearchitecting” its cigarette portfolio and carrying out “a lot of innovations” to tackle challenges arising from sharp tax increases on cigarettes and various tobacco products. Non-cigarette businesses contribute 25% to the bottom line.

articles.headline: Buffett indicator: India's stock market less overheated than others
articles.summary: India's Buffett indicator (market cap to nominal GDP) moderated to 111.6% by March 2026, down from 156% in September 2024. This indicates India's market is less overvalued compared to major equity markets like the US (214.7%), Taiwan (363%), Japan (178.4%) and South Korea (155%).

articles.headline: Ashok Leyland ties up with Shriram Automall for commercial vehicles
articles.summary: Ashok Leyland signed a memorandum of understanding with Shriram Automall India to establish a robust and transparent ecosystem for certified pre-owned commercial vehicles, aiming to streamline the market for used commercial vehicles.

articles.headline: Hyundai Q2 operating profit falls 21%, misses estimates
articles.summary: Hyundai Motor reported a 21% fall in Q2 operating profit to 2.9 trillion won ($1.98 billion), missing analysts’ estimates. This decline was attributed to weaker vehicle sales, production disruptions, and higher costs, despite some support from a weaker won.

articles.headline: ITC aims for No. 1 FMCG player, profitability push: Sanjiv Puri
articles.summary: Diversified conglomerate ITC, aspiring to be India’s No. 1 FMCG player, is taking steps to improve profitability for its non-cigarette FMCG business along with growth. The company expects its addressable FMCG market to reach ₹8 lakh crore by 2035, indicating immense headroom.

articles.headline: OHM losses, cash flow weakness impact Ashok Leyland FY26 profit
articles.summary: Ashok Leyland’s consolidated net profit rose 11.7% in FY26. However, its electric-bus business, OHM Mobility Solutions, saw net loss widen to ₹35.69 crore. Operating cash inflow fell 38.7%, and a ₹1,234.24-crore charge was booked for Hinduja Leyland Finance loan valuation.

articles.headline: Mercedes-Benz: All vehicles sold since January are E25 compatible
articles.summary: Santosh Iyer, MD and CEO of Mercedes-Benz India, confirmed that all vehicles sold since January 2026 are E25-compatible. The entire portfolio has been E20-compliant since 2020, assuring customers about the future of ethanol-blended fuels and fuel-related issues.

articles.headline: JLR previews Range Rover GT electric grand tourer
articles.summary: Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) previewed the Range Rover GT, an all-new battery-electric fastback grand tourer, expanding its portfolio to five core vehicle lines. It's the first model based on the Electrified Modular Architecture (EMA), designed for long-distance comfort and electric performance.

articles.headline: India, Panama to enhance maritime connectivity, port investments
articles.summary: India and Panama are discussing enhancing maritime connectivity and investments in ports. This comes amid West Asia tensions and India's efforts to diversify energy supplies and trade routes. Discussions also covered developing new shipping services to connect markets in North America and Latin America.

articles.headline: HPCL under-recovery on fuel, LPG hits ₹26,000 crore in Q1
articles.summary: HPCL reported ₹26,000 crore in under-recoveries in Q1FY27 on petrol, diesel, and LPG due to the West Asia conflict pushing crude prices up while PSU OMCs sold below market rates. LPG loss stood at ₹510 per cylinder, and debt increased by ₹25,000 crore.

articles.headline: CPCL posts ₹1,031 crore Q1 profit on stronger refining margins
articles.summary: Chennai Petroleum Corporation Ltd (CPCL) reported a consolidated net profit of ₹1,031 crore for Q1FY27, against a net loss last year. Revenue rose 57% to ₹29,359 crore, driven by stronger refining economics and favorable product pricing, despite challenges like increased crude costs and sourcing constraints.

articles.headline: BPCL: No discounts on Russian oil for September deliveries
articles.summary: Bharat Petroleum Corporation (BPCL) stated traders have stopped offering discounts on Russian crude oil for September deliveries, amidst Brent prices surpassing $100/barrel due to the Houthi attacks. BPCL diversified crude sourcing in Q1FY27, with Russian crude making up 38% of total procurement.

articles.headline: India faces WTO scrutiny over tariffs, MSP, quality control
articles.summary: India faced sustained scrutiny from several WTO members during its eighth trade policy review over "abrupt tariff changes," quality control measures, agricultural restrictions (MSP regime, public stockholding), and export restrictions on commodities. Members sought greater clarity on consultation procedures and regulatory certainty.

articles.headline: Pitfalls of FCNR (B) scheme undermine external account stability
articles.summary: The FCNR(B) scheme, designed to attract NRI dollar deposits and strengthen reserves, implicitly promises an exchange rate trajectory. High interest rates, leverage, and the central bank bearing hedging costs raise concerns about the central bank’s balance sheet and increasing dollar dependence, potentially mortgaging future rupee strength.

articles.headline: Gen Z Cockroach Janata Party protests demand NEET accountability
articles.summary: The CJP-led Gen Z protests, sparked by alleged NEET-UG exam leaks and student suicides, demand Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation, compensation, and exam reforms. The movement, leveraging satirical memes for traction, gained 25.6 million Instagram followers. Prime Minister Modi has vowed fast-track courts for culprits.

articles.headline: Risks in financing AI: Financial guardrails needed to curb exuberance
articles.summary: The AI boom, largely funded by borrowed money, poses financial stability risks including concentration, herding, cyber, and opaque model risks. BIS warns AI valuations may be stretched. Governments and regulators, including India's proposed risk-based law, must build stronger financial oversight, diversified supply chains, and resilient energy infrastructure.

articles.headline: Reshaping engineering education for future national missions
articles.summary: India's engineering education needs reform to prepare students for future national missions like agricultural advancements, medical innovations, mobility solutions, manufacturing development, and energy progress. Current curricula, based on 19th-century frameworks, are becoming obsolete and require mission-driven, interdisciplinary programs spanning from research to undergraduate training.

articles.headline: China clings to austerity despite sharper economic dip
articles.summary: China's public spending plunged 11.9% last month, indicating continued fiscal tightening despite increasing calls for more support amid a sharper-than-expected economic slowdown. The broad deficit for the first half was 13% less than a year earlier, with fiscal pullback dragging on overall investment.

articles.headline: Auto component makers target ₹70,300 crore investment over 3 years
articles.summary: Auto component manufacturers have lined up ₹70,300 crore in projects for commissioning between FY27 and FY29, part of a larger ₹4.76 lakh crore pipeline. This signals a shift towards technology-led expansion in EVs and powertrain-agnostic components, driven by policy incentives and localization initiatives.

articles.headline: Bangladesh payments reduce Adani Power's nearly $400 million dues
articles.summary: Adani Power’s receivables from its Bangladesh power supply business stood at nearly $400 million at June-end, but regular payments from Bangladesh Power Development Board (BPDB) are helping reduce outstanding dues. The company expects monthly $100 million payments to continue, improving liquidity realization over time.

articles.headline: NCLT stays Byju’s insolvency bidding process until August end
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has stayed the insolvency bidding process of Byju's parent company Think & Learn till the next hearing on August 31. This provides temporary relief to the company's founders and directs the resolution professional not to proceed with certain steps.

articles.headline: Adani Power mulls first nuclear project in Madhya Pradesh
articles.summary: Adani Power is evaluating the possibility of developing its first nuclear power project at Bina in Madhya Pradesh, while also assessing land at Bina and Nigrie for thermal or nuclear expansion. The Adani Group aims to develop 10 GW of nuclear generation capacity by 2035.

articles.headline: FACT halts ammonium sulphate production due to supply chain woes
articles.summary: Public sector Fertilisers and Chemicals Travancore Ltd (FACT) suspended ammonium sulphate production following supply chain disruptions and soaring raw material costs triggered by geopolitical developments in West Asia. The Centre has increased fertilizer imports to ensure uninterrupted availability for farmers.

articles.headline: Football fever heads to S-E Asia as Asean Championship kicks off
articles.summary: The Asean Championship, running from July 24 to August 26, is showcasing the passionate football culture in Southeast Asia. The tournament involves 10 teams across nine countries, with Vietnam as defending champions. Discussions around naturalization of foreign-born players are a growing topic.

articles.headline: Blinkit to open gourmet dark stores in top 8 cities
articles.summary: Blinkit plans to launch gourmet dark stores in select locations across India's top eight cities to capitalize on the premiumization trend. Eternal, Blinkit's owner, invested ₹3,000 crore over four years to build its network, with quick-commerce net order value growing 86% y-o-y in Q1FY27.

articles.headline: Modi vows fast-track courts to punish exam leak culprits
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi pledged fast-track courts for "swift and stringent punishment" to those involved in exam leaks, responding to the CJP-led Gen Z protests over NEET leaks. Opposition and protestors are demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation, with parliamentary talks stalled.

articles.headline: Gen Z activism uses 'meme-first' approach for exam leak protests
articles.summary: The CJP-led youth movement, protesting exam leaks, effectively uses a "meme-first" approach on platforms like Instagram. Garnering 25.6 million followers, this visual, satirical strategy resonates with Gen Z, contrasting with traditional political campaigns. Observers see it as an internet-first movement influencing political discourse.

articles.headline: Internet suspended, heavy security in central Delhi amid protests
articles.summary: Central Delhi was placed under heavy security with internet services suspended in areas like Jantar Mantar and Sansad Marg, and commercial establishments asked to shut early. This followed clashes during CJP-led protests against exam leaks, disrupting metro services and daily life across the national capital.

articles.headline: Kerala to set up AI-powered ‘watchtower’ for overseas jobs
articles.summary: Kerala CM VD Satheesan announced setting up an AI-powered 'watchtower' to identify global job opportunities for the State's youth. Experts will submit periodic reports to guide curriculum restructuring, aligning academic programs with future employment. AI will also be leveraged for governance and disaster management.

articles.headline: PM Surya Ghar scheme to surpass 50 lakh households soon
articles.summary: New & Renewable Energy Minister Pralhad Joshi announced that the PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojna (PMSG: MBY) is expected to cover over 50 lakh households in the coming weeks. Launched in February 2024 with an outlay of ₹75,021 crore, it is the world's largest domestic rooftop solar program.

articles.headline: IBS Group launches Naviq Technology, new travel AI company in Kochi
articles.summary: Thiruvananthapuram-based IBS Group launched Naviq Technology, a new AI company for the global travel sector, with a $500 million investment. Based in Kochi's Infopark, Naviq aims to use data for predictive tools, starting with 2,000 employees and plans to scale to 5,000, aligning with Kerala's vision to be an AI hub.

articles.headline: Copper prices to see volatility on West Asia supply risks
articles.summary: Copper prices are expected to remain volatile in the coming months due to macro headwinds, US tariff uncertainty, and geopolitical risks in West Asia. Tight inventories, strong import demand, and risks of sulphuric acid shortages from the conflict are supporting elevated prices, despite China's resistance to higher prices.

articles.headline: Copper dips as geopolitical tensions soar
articles.summary: Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange fell 0.6% to $13,725.50 a tonne, tracking equity markets lower after oil prices surged due to escalating West Asia hostilities. Thin inventories of 107,850 tonnes, the lowest since January, provided some underlying support to prices.

articles.headline: Gold falls as US-Iran crisis fuels inflation fears
articles.summary: Spot gold dropped 2.1% to $4,041.59 per ounce, retreating from a two-week high, as the West Asia conflict drove energy prices higher. This fuelled inflation concerns and reinforced expectations of US interest rate hikes this year. US gold futures also slid 2.6% to $4,043.50.

articles.headline: Egg prices hit record high as heat, feed costs bite
articles.summary: Egg prices reached an all-time high in Namakkal, India's largest egg-producing hub, due to prolonged summer heat reducing production, coupled with soaring feed costs for maize (₹28/kg) and soybean (₹71/kg). The supply-demand mismatch, exacerbated by increased mortality of layer birds, could keep prices elevated.

articles.headline: Palm oil hits 3-month high on supply risks
articles.summary: Malaysian palm oil futures closed higher on Thursday, hitting a 3-month high of 4,709 ringgit ($1,152.19) a tonne, on expectations of tighter supply and stronger demand from the biodiesel sector. El Niño risks, lower production forecasts for Q4 2026/H1 2027, and West Asia tensions contribute to the surge.

articles.headline: Maize acreage up in MP, may offset decline in South
articles.summary: Madhya Pradesh reported a 23% jump in maize acreage this kharif season, partly offsetting declines in key producing States like Karnataka and Maharashtra due to rainfall deficit. Overall maize acreage is down about 5% nationally, raising concerns over feed security for the livestock sector.

articles.headline: South Gujarat deluge shatters records, new 'low' looms over Bay
articles.summary: South Gujarat experienced record rainfall, with Umergam receiving 106 cm in 24 hours, due to a rare convergence of Arabian Sea moisture. Another low-pressure area is expected to form over the Bay of Bengal, seeded by typhoon remnants, threatening renewed heavy rain across East India and Western Ghats.

articles.headline: PAC raps Defence Ministry over foreign OEM offset commitment gaps
articles.summary: Parliament’s Public Accounts Committee (PAC) sharply criticized the Ministry of Defence (MoD) over persistent defaults and procedural lapses in implementing the Defence Offsets Policy, with 45% of obligations unfulfilled. It urged making defence offsets universal and focusing on attracting high-tech Transfer of Technology, not just production.

articles.headline: Parliamentary panel urges FSSAI to fast-track front-of-pack rules
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel urged FSSAI to finalize and notify "Front-of-Pack Nutrition Labelling" regulations in a time-bound manner, especially for baby products. It demanded clear declarations of added sugar (grams per serving, % of daily energy) and criticized reliance on numerical disclosures, advocating for easily understandable warnings at the point of purchase.

articles.headline: Viyona gets BBPS nod for bill payment services
articles.summary: Fintech infrastructure start-up Viyona received approval from Bharat Bill Payment System (BBPS) to operate as a customer operating unit. This certification enables Viyona to offer BBPS-powered bill payment services across various categories like electricity, water, gas, broadband, mobile recharges, and insurance premiums.

articles.headline: GMR Hyderabad Airport launches dedicated premium taxi service
articles.summary: GMR Hyderabad International Airport has launched a dedicated premium airport taxi service, available 24/7 with a fleet of over 200 vehicles. This service aims to ensure seamless transportation for passengers, including during peak hours and holidays, delivering a premium, airport-assured travel experience.

articles.headline: Centre red-flags 2.91 crore suspect ration card beneficiaries
articles.summary: The Centre identified 2.91 crore suspect beneficiaries under various red-flag categories, including 5.60 lakh over 100 years old, 2.11 lakh single-member, 2.20 lakh silent, 30.06 lakh duplicate, and 34.12 lakh deceased. It urged time-bound verification while taking utmost care to prevent wrongful exclusion of eligible beneficiaries.

articles.headline: AP clears plan for first private women’s university
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh Cabinet approved the establishment of Vishnu Women’s University, making it the first private women’s university in the State. This involved clearing a proposal to promulgate an ordinance amending the Andhra Pradesh Private Universities (Establishment and Regulation) Act, 2016.

articles.headline: West Asia crisis drags down Coromandel Intl Q1 PAT by 24%
articles.summary: Coromandel International Ltd's Q1 net profit fell 24% to ₹382 crore, despite a 15% rise in total income to ₹8,215 crore. The West Asia conflict sharply escalated raw material prices, while subsidy rates remained inadequate to fully offset increased input costs, impacting fertilizer segment margins.

articles.headline: JSW’s ₹600-crore dry bulk terminal at VOC Port ready by Q4
articles.summary: JSW Infrastructure's ₹600-crore dry bulk cargo terminal mechanization project at Thoothukudi's VOC Port is expected to be completed by Q4FY27. The project, involving mechanization of North Cargo Berth-III with a 7 mtpa handling capacity, will boost the port’s dry bulk trade and attract larger vessels.

articles.headline: J&K aims to double start-ups by 2027
articles.summary: Jammu and Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah stated the government aims to double the number of start-ups in the Union Territory to 2,600-2,800 by 2027. He emphasized creating an enabling environment for entrepreneurship, encouraging industry leaders to work with local entrepreneurs, and attracting investment through seed support.

articles.headline: ServiceNow invests $40 million in Businessnext at $700 million valuation
articles.summary: Global tech major ServiceNow invested $40 million in Businessnext, an India-focused banking and financial services AI platform, at a $700 million Series C valuation. This strengthens efforts to drive autonomous operations and private AI solutions, boosting India's role in global AI technology for financial services.

articles.headline: IndiGo MD flags concerns over proposal allowing airports to own airlines
articles.summary: IndiGo MD Rahul Bhatia expressed reservations about a government proposal allowing airport operators to own airlines, cautioning it would create "significant conflicts of interest" and work against consumer interests. The Ministry of Civil Aviation is reviewing regulatory reforms to simplify market entry and broaden private participation in the sector.

articles.headline: SpiceJet adds three Airbus A320 aircraft to its fleet
articles.summary: SpiceJet inducted three Airbus A320 aircraft into its fleet under damp lease arrangements, with more planned as part of its fleet augmentation program. These additions will support network expansion and enhance connectivity across domestic routes, improving operational flexibility and cost efficiency, ahead of the busy travel season.

articles.headline: CCI junks complaint over Zomato pricing practices
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) dismissed a complaint against Eternal (owner of Zomato) regarding alleged abuse of dominance and inflated pricing on its food delivery app. The complainant claimed a food item purchased through Zomato cost ₹198 against ₹105 directly. The CCI found no prima facie case.

articles.headline: Cipla Q1 net profit slips 39%; diversified model to weather US tariffs
articles.summary: Cipla's Q1 net profit fell 39% to ₹789 crore (vs. ₹1,298 crore last year), primarily due to lower revenues from specific products. Managing Director Achin Gupta stated its highly diversified US model, with 35-40% manufacturing in the US, helps weather proposed US tariffs on generic drugs.

articles.headline: इन्फोसिस ने आशीष दास को चुना नया सीईओ
articles.summary: Infosys has appointed Ashish Kumar Das as its new CEO, effective April 1, 2027, succeeding Salil Parekh. Das, who has spent over three decades at Infosys, is currently the global head of diversified enterprise portfolios. His immediate appointment is as CEO-designate.

articles.headline: ईयू ने गूगल पर लगाया 1 अरब डॉलर का जुर्माना
articles.summary: The European Union (EU) fined Google €890 million (approx. $1 billion) for violating digital competition rules. Google is accused of operating its Play Store and search engine in ways that favor its own services and apps, harming competitors. This is one of the largest actions against a big tech company.

articles.headline: इन्फोसिस ने आय अनुमान घटाया
articles.summary: Infosys has trimmed its revenue growth forecast for the current fiscal year to 1.5-3%, down from the previous 1.5-3.5% range. In Q1 FY27, its net profit grew 12.2% to ₹7,769 crore, with revenue up 14% to ₹48,211 crore, but performance was below analyst estimates.

articles.headline: विदेशी स्वामित्व वाली ई-कॉमर्स फर्मों को निर्यात के लिए स्टॉक बनाने की मंजूरी
articles.summary: The Indian government has allowed foreign-owned e-commerce firms to hold inventory for export purposes. This move benefits major US e-commerce companies like Amazon and Walmart-owned Flipkart. FDI in inventory-based e-commerce for domestic sales remains restricted.

articles.headline: बीपीसीएल बढ़ा सकती है तेल के दाम
articles.summary: BPCL may raise fuel prices if crude oil remains above $90/barrel, driven by US-Iran conflict and supply disruptions. The company has contacted the government regarding losses from petrol, diesel, and LPG sales. BPCL is considering a term contract with the US for crude supply.

articles.headline: फ्लिपकार्ट ऑनलाइन फूड डिलीवरी बाजार में देगी दस्तक
articles.summary: Flipkart, owned by Walmart, plans to enter the online food delivery market, starting a pilot project in Bengaluru next month. The service will initially launch on ONDC, later introducing its own app, aiming to cater to diverse customer segments and compete with existing players.

articles.headline: भारत की आजादी के बाद आर्थिक सुधारों की यात्रा
articles.summary: This article reflects on India's economic reforms post-1991. While per capita income has quadrupled, and poverty significantly reduced, challenges remain in areas like health, education, and social indicators, with India still lagging many countries in human development.

articles.headline: सुधारों में देरी से युवाओं के भविष्य को नुकसान
articles.summary: Montek Singh Ahluwalia argues that delays in India's economic reforms have negatively impacted the future of its youth. He stresses the need for continued reforms, warning that incomplete changes could jeopardize the country's growth trajectory and job creation.

articles.headline: अल्टाटेक सीमेंट जुटाएगी 5,000 करोड़ रुपये
articles.summary: UltraTech Cement's board has approved raising ₹5,000 crore through non-convertible debentures (NCDs). The funds will be raised in one or more tranches via private placement, with each debenture valued at ₹1 lakh.

articles.headline: वारी रिन्यूएबल का शुद्ध लाभ 38% बढ़ा
articles.summary: Waaree Renewable Technologies reported a 37.7% year-on-year rise in consolidated net profit to ₹118.97 crore for Q1 FY27. Operational income grew 53.23% to ₹924.25 crore in the same period.

articles.headline: आईटीसी की रणनीति: सिगरेट पोर्टफोलियो में बदलाव
articles.summary: ITC Chairman Sanjiv Puri states the company is adopting a calibrated pricing strategy and diversifying its portfolio to mitigate losses from higher cigarette taxes. The company aims for innovation across its product categories.

articles.headline: मारुति सुजुकी की 'ब्रेजा' अब नए लुक में
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki is launching its Brezza compact SUV in a new look to attract a broader customer base, including first-time buyers and those upgrading. The design aims to cater to evolving customer preferences in the competitive SUV market.

articles.headline: पीवीआर आईनॉक्स ने दर्ज शुद्ध लाभ
articles.summary: PVR INOX reported a consolidated net profit of ₹56.5 crore for Q1 FY27, a turnaround from a net loss of ₹47.3 crore in the same quarter last year. Operational revenue increased to ₹1,622.2 crore.

articles.headline: इंडिगो ने पेश किया ग्राहक सुविधा सुधार प्रस्ताव
articles.summary: IndiGo has proposed measures to the Competition Commission of India (CCI) to mitigate the impact of "mass disruptions" due to flight cancellations. These include offering alternative flights within 48-72 hours without extra charges or fare differences for affected passengers.

articles.headline: पहली तिमाही में सिप्ला का मुनाफा 39% लुढ़का
articles.summary: Cipla reported a 39% year-on-year drop in consolidated net profit to ₹789 crore in Q1 FY27, despite a 2.3% rise in operational revenue to ₹7,119 crore. The decline is attributed to a high base effect from strong US business performance last year.

articles.headline: वेदांत की हाईकोर्ट के फैसले के खिलाफ अपील
articles.summary: Vedanta Oil & Gas has appealed to the NCLAT against a Delhi High Court single-judge bench ruling that upheld the government's decision not to extend its contract for the CB-OS/2 offshore oil and gas block in Gujarat.

articles.headline: मर्सिडीज-बेंज को त्योहारी मांग की उम्मीद
articles.summary: Mercedes-Benz India expects strong sales during the festive season, anticipating a robust performance in Q1 2026 with 10% growth. The company aims for its best year, with EVs accounting for 14% of sales.

articles.headline: बैजू को अंतरिम राहत मिली
articles.summary: The Bengaluru NCLT has temporarily halted the Byju's insolvency resolution process until August 31, granting relief to the founders. The RP was directed not to issue Form G (inviting bids) for potential buyers, following a challenge by founders against lenders' claims.

articles.headline: लंदन स्टॉक एक्सचेंज पर सूचीबद्ध होगी एयरटेल मनी
articles.summary: Airtel Africa plans to list its mobile money arm, Airtel Money, on the London Stock Exchange in the second half of the year. This move aims to reach a wider international investor base and support its ambitious financial services expansion in Africa.

articles.headline: अधिकारी न करें जमीन के मुआवजे पर फैसला
articles.summary: The Supreme Court questioned the National Highways Act, 1956 provision allowing government-appointed officers to decide land acquisition compensation disputes. The court stated that judicial functions, like determining compensation, should be handled by a judicially trained forum.

articles.headline: टीटीजेड प्राधिकरण को ताजमहल के आसपास उद्योगों पर कार्रवाई की अनुमति
articles.summary: The Supreme Court allowed the Taj Trapezium Zone Authority to process pending applications for industries near the Taj Mahal. The court noted delays in environmental studies should not indefinitely halt decisions, directing a committee of experts to review the matters.

articles.headline: छोटे उद्यमों के मुनाफे पर असर
articles.summary: A report indicates that quality control orders (QCOs) disproportionately affect small firms, reducing their profitability by 47.6% due to higher costs, while having a lesser impact on larger companies.

articles.headline: आईएमएफ का वित्तीय सुरक्षा बढ़ाने का आग्रह
articles.summary: The IMF urged policymakers to strengthen AI oversight in finance, warning inadequate governance could raise systemic and cyber risks in trading and lending. It noted India's rapid digital adoption requires robust technical skills for AI assessment.

articles.headline: रेलवे का माल ढुलाई में हिस्सा 40% करने का लक्ष्य
articles.summary: Indian Railways aims to increase its share in the country's freight transport from 24% to 40% in the next 5-10 years. This target will be achieved through various reforms, including expediting land acquisition and environmental clearances before project approval.

articles.headline: बाजार में कच्चे तेल के उछाल से शेयर बाजार की बिगड़ी चाल
articles.summary: Indian benchmark equity indices fell for the fourth consecutive day due to rising crude oil prices and continued FPI selling. Nifty dropped 0.53% to 23,870, while Sensex lost 0.47% to 76,391.38. Rising oil prices pose risks to inflation, trade deficit, and corporate earnings.

articles.headline: सऊदी टैंकरों पर हूती हमलों से कच्चा तेल $100 पर पहुंचा
articles.summary: Crude oil prices surged to a one-month high, nearing $100 per barrel, after Yemen's Houthi rebels claimed attacks on two Saudi oil tankers. This escalates tensions in the Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz, disrupting oil transit and fueling inflation fears.

articles.headline: तेल में तेजी से महंगाई बढ़ने की आशंका, सोना 2% टूटा
articles.summary: Gold prices fell nearly 2% amid rising crude oil prices and inflation concerns, strengthening expectations of higher US interest rates. Bond yields increased, making non-yielding assets like gold less attractive to investors.

articles.headline: एमएफ ऑनली पीएमएस का निवेश दायरा
articles.summary: SEBI proposed significant changes to Portfolio Manager Services (PMS) rules, including introducing MF-only PMS with a lower minimum investment of ₹25 lakh. It allows PMS to invest in foreign securities, exchange-traded derivatives, and unlisted securities to broaden investment avenues and offer customized solutions.

articles.headline: भारत में क्यों नहीं नवाचार पर जोर?
articles.summary: This editorial questions why India's large companies and business groups aren't prioritizing innovation. It suggests they might feel less competitive pressure due to strong balance sheets and market dominance, leading to less investment in R&D and new technologies compared to smaller firms.

articles.headline: 1991 के आर्थिक सुधारों के सबक
articles.summary: This editorial revisits the 1991 economic reforms, highlighting how they transformed India's economy from a crisis to sustained growth. It emphasizes the bold decisions taken by then-PM PV Narasimha Rao and FM Manmohan Singh, which laid the foundation for liberalization.

articles.headline: चलती ट्रेन में गिरफ्तारी और एक बैंक घोटाले की कहानी
articles.summary: This article details the dramatic arrest of a bank official involved in a ₹16 crore fraud. The official exploited dormant accounts and premature FDs, using shared passwords to bypass internal controls, highlighting vulnerabilities in banking systems.

articles.headline: कमजोर मॉनसून के आर्थिक प्रभाव को नजरअंदाज नहीं किया जा सकता
articles.summary: A reader highlights RBI Governor's concern about weak monsoon impacting economic growth, especially agriculture, rural income, and inflation. Calls for irrigation expansion, water conservation, crop diversification, and crop insurance to mitigate risks.

articles.headline: नीट पेपर लीक पर उचित सरकारी कदम की मांग
articles.summary: A reader expresses anger over persistent paper leaks in exams like NEET, leading to student protests. Urges the government to implement strict rules and take swift action against corruption to address student grievances and prevent future occurrences.

articles.headline: स्वदेश निर्मित लंबी दूरी की मिसाइल 'विशा' का सफल परीक्षण
articles.summary: DRDO successfully test-fired the indigenous long-range surface-to-air missile 'Visha' off the Odisha coast. This marks a significant step towards self-reliance in India's air defense capabilities.

articles.headline: पोषक तत्वं वाले लेबल नियमों को जल्द अंतिम रूप दे नियामक
articles.summary: A parliamentary committee urged FSSAI to finalize food labeling rules by September 13, 2022, to ensure clear nutritional information for consumers, especially on sugar content in highly processed foods, noting significant delays.

articles.headline: असम में बाढ़ से 6.43 लाख लोग प्रभावित
articles.summary: Floods continue to worsen in Assam, affecting over 6.43 lakh people in 10 districts. CM Himanta Biswa Sarma confirmed central government support, with a central team to assess damages. The death toll from floods has reached 41.

articles.headline: सिक्किम सुरंग हादसे में 25 श्रमिकों के शव बरामद
articles.summary: All 25 laborers trapped in the collapsed tunnel of NHPC's Teesta Stage-6 hydropower project in Sikkim's Namchi district have been recovered. The tunnel collapsed due to a suspected methane gas explosion on Monday. Compensation announced by NHPC and state/central governments.

articles.headline: नीट पर्चा लीक पर फास्ट ट्रैक कोर्ट: मोदी
articles.summary: PM Modi announced the formation of fast-track courts to investigate the NEET paper leak, amidst ongoing protests. He assured strong action against those involved, following clashes between protesters and police. Government representatives have called for dialogue with protesting students.

articles.headline: ईरान पर अमेरिकी हमले जारी
articles.summary: US forces continued airstrikes on Iran for the 12th consecutive night, intensifying tensions over control of crucial sea trade routes. This follows claims by Yemen's Houthi rebels, backed by Iran, of attacking two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, escalating regional conflict.

articles.headline: पूर्वी एशिया संकट, रूस-यूक्रेन युद्ध का छाया मुद्दा
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar, at the East Asia Summit in Manila, called for diplomacy to resolve the West Asia conflict and the Russia-Ukraine war, emphasizing the need for safe and unobstructed international sea lanes. He met Russian FM Lavrov amidst global instability.

articles.headline: खेती में अब नई सोच अपनाने की जरूरत: अशोक लाहिरी, डॉ. एस. महेंद्र देव
articles.summary: Ashok Lahiri and Dr. S. Mahendra Dev of EAC-PM advocated for new thinking in agriculture, promoting diversification, post-harvest management, better marketing, and agro-processing. They emphasized non-farm employment and adopting modern tech like AI for farmer income growth.

articles.headline: जगन्नाथ मंदिर की भूमि वापस लेगी ओडिशा सरकार
articles.summary: The Odisha government initiated a major drive to reclaim over 60,426 acres of Jagannath Temple land, including 395.252 acres outside the state, from encroachments and legal disputes. A dedicated corpus of ₹10,000 crore will be established for temple land management.

articles.headline: More than 9 lakh affected in Assam
articles.summary: Assam is facing unprecedented floods affecting over 7.21 lakh people, with six deaths reported on Thursday, bringing the toll to 47. Three eastern districts, Sivasagar, Charaideo, and Jorhat, which have not historically flooded, are the worst hit. CM Himanta Biswa Sarma attributes the devastation to cloudbursts upstream and rainfall 436% above normal.

articles.headline: Explosives used in Sikkim tunnel accident: CM
articles.summary: The death toll in the Sikkim hydropower tunnel disaster has risen to 25 after all trapped workers' bodies were recovered. Chief Minister Prem Singh Tamang stated that explosives were used, and methane gas caused the blast. NHPC and the state government are conducting separate investigations.

articles.headline: LeT terrorists’ houses demolished in J&K
articles.summary: Security forces demolished the houses of two LeT terrorists, Adil Ahmad Thoker and Haroon Rashid Ganai, in Anantnag following a policeman's killing. Locals alleged the blast also damaged a mosque and vehicle. Authorities stated the demolitions were against Supreme Court directions.

articles.headline: India makes short work of Zimbabwe
articles.summary: India defeated Zimbabwe by seven wickets in the first T20I, with opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi becoming the youngest to hit a T20I half-century at 15 years. Mayank Yadav's bowling also played a crucial role in restricting Zimbabwe to 125/7.

articles.headline: Houthi attacks on 2 Saudi Arabia-flagged tankers drive Brent crude price past $100
articles.summary: Houthis attacked two Saudi tankers in the Red Sea, causing Brent crude prices to surge past $100 a barrel. This escalation off the Bab el-Mandeb Strait impacts India's oil imports, forcing rerouting via longer, costlier routes.

articles.headline: PM reaches out to students; Centre silent on use of force
articles.summary: PM Modi announced fast-track courts for paper leak cases, promising action against those harming youth's future. However, the Centre and Delhi Police remain silent on the alleged use of force on protesters, with the Delhi HC seeking a response.

articles.headline: Mamata’s rally: FIR for ‘violation’ of HC order
articles.summary: Kolkata police filed an FIR against Mamata Banerjee for her rally allegedly violating a HC order limiting road blockages. The rally exceeded attendance limits, and the court deferred Abhishek Banerjee's plea for protection.

articles.headline: All roads continue to lead to Jantar Mantar
articles.summary: Thousands protested at Jantar Mantar despite metro and internet curbs. CJP founder alleged BJP 'goons' infiltrated protests. Shops in Connaught Place closed early due to security concerns. Police stopped students in autorickshaws, citing prohibitory orders.

articles.headline: Students see ‘familiar playbook’ as DU, colleges endorse govt. action
articles.summary: Delhi University and colleges are advising students to avoid Jantar Mantar, endorsing government actions. Students and faculty view these responses as a 'familiar playbook', criticizing the administration's perceived indifference.

articles.headline: Police say two ACPs injured in clashes; FIR registered
articles.summary: Two Assistant Commissioners of Police were injured during protests near Jantar Mantar, leading to an FIR. Police used tear gas to disperse the crowd, alleging involvement of 'anti-social elements'. At least six personnel were injured.

articles.headline: Don’t let Opposition hijack students’ protest: Delhi BJP
